title: abutilon indicum var. guineense
intro: Abutilon indicum var. guineense, also known as the Indian mallow, is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive leaf shape. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, providing health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as used in topical applications and ointments, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
